text:
I've been thinking about this, and I realize I owe you a genuine apology. What I said the other day was careless, and when I saw how it landed, I felt the weight of it immediately. I'm sorry.

I didn't intend to hurt you, but I also know that intentions don't erase the sting of words that came out wrong, or thoughtlessly, or at the wrong moment. You deserved more consideration from me than I gave you. You've always been generous with your time and your trust, and I repaid that poorly, and I regret it.

I've been sitting with it because I wanted this to be more than just a quick "sorry" that asks you to move on before you're ready. You don't have to accept this right away, and you don't have to pretend everything is fine. I just want you to know that I see what I did, I'm not making excuses for it, and your feelings make complete sense to me.

If there's anything I can do to make things right, I want to. And if you need space first, that's okay too. What matters most to me is that you know I value you, that I take what happened seriously, and that I'm genuinely trying to do better.
